research_papers.title: Advancing graph-supported machine learning in generative design for architectural engineering
research_papers.abstract: Total number of GNN papers reviewed is 59. The limitations and future directions from the GNN papers have been grouped into key categories, with generalized descriptions to capture the overarching themes without oversimplification. The themes (for future directions) include ‘ data limitations ’, ‘ poor generalization to new or complex structures ’, ‘ narrow application scope ’, ‘ high computational demands ’, ‘ limited integration with human experts ’, ‘ underutilization of advanced technologies ’, ‘ unstable predictions and lack of physical constraints ’, ‘ ineffective feature fusion and edge utilization ’, and ‘ lack of objective model evaluation metrics ’.

research_papers.title: Video Captioning using Deep Learning with Greedy Search (VCDLGS)
research_papers.abstract: Limitations and Future Research: Despite significant advances, several limitations persist: (1) performance degradation on videos exceeding 15-word captions, (2) challenges in fine-grained object discrimination (animal types, similar actions), (3) limited temporal reasoning for complex multi-event sequences, and (4) inherent black-box nature of CNN components limiting full interpretability. These limitations provide clear directions for future research.

research_papers.title: The emerging portrait of GDNF: A small neuroinductive isoform named djGDNF47
research_papers.abstract: Although several clinical trials demonstrated motor function improvements in a subset of patients, the overall efficacy of GDNF as a therapeutic agent for Parkinson's disease has not matched the robust results observed in preclinical animal models. It should be noted, however, that in a subsequent postmortem analysis, neuronal outgrowth formation was detected in neurons in delivery. With successful preclinical studies in rodents and primates, it eventually became evident that little is known about GDNF to be able to understand the reason for failure in clinical trials. Failure has been attributed to a number of factors, including poor delivery of GDNF to the required areas and cells, alteration of the factor's functionality when produced in Escherichia coli, and the use of the wrong GDNF isoform. Although preclinical studies have demonstrated promising outcomes, subsequent investigations aimed at elucidating the reasons for limited clinical success have highlighted several contributing factors. These include the association of the protein with the cell membrane, its immunogenic properties, and structural instability, all of which may hinder therapeutic efficacy. In addition to molecular limitations, the failure of clinical trials has also been attributed to trial design factors, including administration route, dosage, delivery site, and patient selection. Neurotrophic factor trials often involve patients at advanced stages of Parkinson's disease (PD), due to the invasive and prolonged nature of intracranial delivery. However, at this stage, dopaminergic neuronal loss is extensive, limiting the potential for neuroprotection. Furthermore, degeneration of axonal terminals disrupts retrograde transport, impairing neurotrophic signaling. These challenges may compromise therapeutic outcomes and highlight the importance of re-evaluating GDNF-based interventions. All these assumptions sent researchers back to the fundamental research of this neurotrophic factor.

research_papers.title: A novel Improved Geographically Weighted Random Forest (IGWRF) model for low-resolution soil moisture data downscaling in Africa
research_papers.abstract: Although the IGWRF model proposed in this study demonstrates relatively superior downscaling performance in Kenya, it still presents some limitations in practical application. First, compared with conventional RF, both GWRF and IGWRF have higher computational costs, mainly reflected in longer model-building time. This may stem from the fact that the model needs to calculate local weights and the optimal bandwidth, which prolongs training. This bottleneck is common to many local modeling methods. Future work will focus on reducing computational, cost through model lightweighting. For example, we can reduce the number of trees in the local models to improve computational efficiency.

research_papers.title: Cocaine’s cerebrovascular vasoconstriction is associated with astrocytic Ca 2+  increase in mice
research_papers.abstract: One limitation of our study was that measurements were done in anesthetized mice using isoflurane. Thus, vasodilation from isoflurane might have accentuated the magnitude of cocaine induced vasoconstriction and its anesthetic effects might have attenuated the sensitivity of neurons and perhaps also of astrocytes to cocaine. Thus future studies should evaluate cocaine’s effects in awake animals. Another limitation was that we imaged neurons and astrocytes with GCaMP6f in separate group of animals, which was done to optimize detection of Ca 2+ fluorescence changes as a function of cell type. In other words, it allowed us to avoid using the same gain setting fitted to the stronger fluorescence from one cell type (e.g., from neurons) relative to the weaker ones from another cell type (e.g., from astrocytes). Nevertheless, red-shifted genetically encoded calcium indicator such as jRGECO1a red fluorescence probe could be used with green probes such as GCaMP6f to image the activity and interactions of different cell types such as neurons and astrocytes simultaneously. To do so, custom-designed emission filters will be needed to synchronize with the excitation of GCaMP and jRGECO1a, respectively, so that it can detect images of green Ca 2+ fluorescence from astrocytes and red Ca 2+ fluorescence signal from neurons in the same field of view. Another limitation was the small sample size used in this study driven by laboriousness of the imaging experiments. Nevertheless, we were able to show significant effects of cocaine, which reflects the large effects being measured.

research_papers.title: Improved large-scale multi-objective competitive swarm optimizer with direction learning and decision enhancement
research_papers.abstract: LMOEADS and LSMOF outperform LGCSO on the LMF12 problem. Furthermore, LGCSO exhibits inferior performance on LMF9 - LMF12. This decline in performance may be attributed to the introduction of the inverted PF in LMF9 - LMF12, which increases the complexity of these test functions. Under such conditions, our strategy faces significant challenges. Addressing this limitation represents a critical direction for future research.

research_papers.title: A comprehensive systematic review on machine learning application in the 5G-RAN architecture: Issues, challenges, and future directions
research_papers.abstract: The main limitation of this review lies in the selection of reputable academic databases, such as ScienceDirect, IEEE Xplore, and Web of Science, which may have excluded additional important sources from less frequently used databases. This could have restricted the scope by missing fresh perspectives on 5G-RAN and machine learning applications. Time constraints further limited the review's comprehensiveness, as the rapid development of 5G and machine learning may have led to overlooked recent advancements; the last articles reviewed were from January 2024. Additionally, while the review discusses the theoretical benefits and applications of machine learning in 5G-RAN, real-world validation is necessary. Many studies are based on simulations, so field testing is essential to confirm the efficacy, scalability, and reliability of proposed solutions. Despite these constraints, the review highlights significant recent advancements in the field.

research_papers.title: Enhanced light field depth estimation through occlusion refinement and feature fusion
research_papers.abstract: Limitation Discussion In discussing the limitations of our work, it is important to acknowledge certain aspects where the model's performance, while strong, does not always achieve the top results across all metrics. For instance, in some scenes such as "Dots," LFMCNet achieves suboptimal results. It is designed to test the accuracy of algorithms in segmenting circular edges and assessing the impact of camera noise on objects of different sizes. While LFMCNet effectively handles noise suppression and ensures accurate segmentation of curved boundaries, the prediction of very small circular edges still requires further improvement. Moreover, the current model is primarily trained on the 4D light field dataset. Although LFMCNet performs well in scenes like "Boxes," "Cotton," "Dino," and "Sideboard," this dataset limitation raises concerns regarding its generalization to more diverse or complex environments. The model's ability to handle more challenging and varied scenarios remains an open question. That said, these limitations do not detract from the overall effectiveness of the model. LFMCNet demonstrates robustness and high accuracy in many key areas, and its performance can be seen as a strong foundation.

research_papers.title: Integrating anatomy and function for zebrafish circuit analysis
research_papers.abstract: Whole brain calcium imaging at cellular resolution requires sensitive genetically encoded calcium indicators and homogeneous expression in order to allow activity recording in all neurons of the animal. In a pioneering study, Ahrens et al. reported on brain-wide activity at cellular resolution during a motor learning paradigm. The authors identified activity that was correlated with a visual stimulus, fictive swimming behavior, or with altered motor performance. This study, which elegantly illustrated the power of holistic calcium recording, also revealed some of its limitations. Due to the relatively low sensitivity of the genetically encoded calcium indicator used, GCaMP2 (Tallini et al., ), much of the brain activity was likely missed, and the authors accordingly report that activity could only be detected in about 1% of the cells during the experiments. More recent calcium indicators, like GCaMP5G (Akerboom et al., ) already allow to observe activity in a much larger fraction of neurons. In the study by Akerboom et al., GCaMP5G, GCaMP3, and the synthetic indicator OGB-1-AM were compared side by side in a test of visual responsiveness in mouse cortex. While GCaMP5G was twice as good as GCaMP3 (20 vs. 10% of the expressing cells were visually responsive), it still lagged the performance of the synthetic dye OGB-1-AM, for which almost 40% of the cells responded. A low calcium indicator sensitivity also biases toward detection of large calcium events, while low firing rates of responsive cells are likely missed. In the above mentioned light sheet microscopy study, Ahrens and Keller already used a recent genetically encoded calcium indicator, GCaMP5G, for whole brain imaging, which should allow improved activity detection compared to the previous study with GCaMP2 (Ahrens et al., ). However, the fraction of responsive cells was not reported. Genetically encoded activity probes with improved sensitivities and linear dynamic ranges are in demand and will likely be developed in the near future (Akemann et al., ; Chen et al., ; Muto et al., ). In addition, suitable promoters for the homogeneous pan-neuronal expression need to be identified to avoid differences in expression levels (e.g., the HuC/elavl3 promoter induces only very low levels of expression in the diencephalon at 5 dpf).

research_papers.title: MPCF: Multi-stage progressive cross-modal fusion for depression severity prediction using audio-Visual modalities
research_papers.abstract: The MPCF framework exhibits several limitations that merit consideration. The FPM lacks explicit alignment constraints, which is a common challenge in multimodal fusion studies. This may limit the model’s capacity to exploit subtle audio-visual interactions through implicit learning fully. The exclusion of physiological modalities such as EEG and GSR also constrains the modeling of depression. This omission limits the framework’s capacity to capture neurophysiological markers, such as arousal dysregulation, which may explain residual prediction errors in severe cases where symptoms extend beyond audio-visual signals.

research_papers.title: Text-guided visual prompt learning with semantic prompt generation and feature fusion
research_papers.abstract: To further clarify potential limitations, we provide additional discussion below. SPGFF relies on both full-resolution visual input and fine-grained, category-specific textual descriptions, which are generated in advance by GPT-4. While this setup benefits accuracy and alignment, it also introduces dependencies on high-quality textual descriptions that may not always be available in real-world applications—especially for systems operating on edge devices or under strict latency and memory constraints. Furthermore, the additional architectural complexity of SPGFF, including multi-stage attention mechanisms and text-guided prompt generation, contributes to increased runtime latency and parameter size. These aspects could pose challenges for deployment in memory-limited or latency-sensitive applications.

While this setup benefits accuracy and alignment, it also introduces dependencies on high-quality textual descriptions that may not always be available in real-world applications—especially for systems operating on edge devices or under strict latency and memory constraints. Furthermore, the additional architectural complexity of SPGFF, including multi-stage attention mechanisms and text-guided prompt generation, contributes to increased runtime latency and parameter size. These aspects could pose challenges for deployment in memory-limited or latency-sensitive applications.

research_papers.title: Smart MOF-Integrated Biopolymeric Hydrogel Systems as a Novel Strategy for Sustained Anticancer Drug Delivery
research_papers.abstract: A comparison of representative 5-FU delivery systems reported in the literature is summarized in. As shown, polymeric nanoparticles, lipid–drug conjugates, metallic nanoparticles, and bare MOF carriers typically suffer from one or more limitations such as burst drug release, short release duration, material-associated toxicity, limited mechanical stability, or lack of glioblastoma specificity. These limitations highlight the absence of delivery platforms that simultaneously achieve high drug loading, prolonged release, and biocompatibility suitable for glioblastoma therapy.

research_papers.title: Classification of triple extraction and RDF generation using boosted BERT fused attention convolutional Bi-directional LSTM with optimization
research_papers.abstract: The limitations of the existing works are listed below: Processing large volumes of data incurs significant computational costs and time, leading to a sensitive level of complexity in the overall system. This extended processing duration delays the attainment of immediate results and may contribute to losses, adversely impacting triple classification performance. In comparison to deep learning approaches, machine learning-based techniques exhibit low performance. The handling of text data is ineffective, hampering the accurate extraction of triples and potentially resulting in substantial data losses. Moreover, there is a scarcity of research articles on the RDF triple extraction, and the existing literature often lacks comprehensive explanations of the procedures involved.
